name: Cursor utilities
description: Dictates styling and available actions for visible cursors and pointer/touch actions.

families.name: cursor
families.description: Sets the visual display of the cursor icon.
guidance: Use the appropriate contextual cursor when it provides meaningful information to the end user. 'Pointer' for when the cursor is above interactive (clickable) elements, 'not-allowed' when the element is in a disabled state, 'grab' when an element is draggable, etc. Hyper specific cursors like 'vertical-text' or 'copy' are not necessary - use 'pointer' for a copy button and the default cursor for an input element or when hovering over text content instead.
